summary: Established PR and nightly CI workflows with traceable web and Windows desktop smoke artifacts, added root build:web and ci:artifact-manifest scripts, and added a strict artifact manifest writer that records commit, Node/pnpm versions, lockfile SHA-256, artifact file checksums, and fails when required artifacts are missing or empty. The Spark release_engineer writer produced the initial CI files but ended with an Unsupported content type tool error; lead_orchestrator integrated the partial work, split web and Windows desktop jobs, added Playwright Chromium installation, tightened manifest failure behavior, and verified the gates.

tests_run.result: Scope all 12 workspace projects; already up to date.
Prettier, ESLint, recursive workspace typecheck, and architecture boundaries passed. The new .github workflows and tools/ci script are included in format/lint/typecheck coverage.
Built desktop shell and generated smoke package under .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.
Artifact manifest written; recorded build commit 64c196f241f38a48c0a7714106b89f73e7dcf805, lockfile sha256 9007d3da5bad490de3ecc3df71434fca840bbf5d00106a8ef73e6d2155d2c1db, and 2 artifacts.
Negative test failed as intended with 'Artifact path does not exist', proving missing artifacts do not pass silently.

Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop; renderer file navigation is restricted to packaged renderer root; IPC channels remain desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Architecture boundaries passed; deterministic Node/worker-compatible hash remained 61e0ba1c; root Vitest passed 1 file/2 tests; web Vitest passed 1 file/3 tests.
Storybook v10.4.6 build completed successfully. Vite emitted a chunk-size warning only.
Playwright Chromium ran 1 web shell test and passed. Vite emitted the known dynamic import analysis warning for hello-simulation-adapter.mjs.

decisions: Split CI into Ubuntu web/browser jobs and Windows desktop smoke jobs so the Windows smoke artifact is produced on windows-latest.
Added Playwright Chromium installation in CI before browser smoke.
Uploaded web and desktop smoke artifacts with adjacent JSON manifests containing commit, lockfile hash, tool versions, file checksums, and artifact sizes.
Kept release scope to .github/**, tools/ci/**, and package.json; no product, architecture, or business implementation files were changed.
Used the existing F006 desktop:package smoke output rather than creating a signed installer; signed/release-grade packaging remains post-M0/release scope.
risks: Workflow syntax was validated through formatting and static review, but GitHub Actions was not executed remotely before this local handoff.
desktop:package is still a smoke package, not a signed Windows installer.
Nightly currently mirrors the PR artifact checks; longer simulation/performance/license/SBOM jobs remain future release-hardening work.
requested_action: Review CI workflows, artifact manifest failure behavior, traceability evidence, scope boundaries, and local command evidence. Return ACCEPT / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK.
